channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: One moment, calm waters. The next, a 900-pound dolphin landed on their boat
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 400 kilograms (900 pounds) appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Elissa Slotkin assails Trump's early actions, offers Democrats a way to fight back
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — First-term Michigan Sen. Elissa Slotkin accused President Donald Trump of driving up costs while pushing for an “unprecedented giveaway to his billionaire friends” in Tuesday night's Democratic response to his first joint congressional address of his second term.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Rep. Al Green shouts down Trump and may face censure by the House for the outburst
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic Rep. Al Green of Texas wasn’t the first lawmaker ever to blurt out a shout of protest during a presidential address to Congress. But he's perhaps the only one in recent memory to actually be ejected from the hall Tuesday night by the Speaker of the House.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Democrats disrupt, protest and wear pained expressions for Trump’s speech
item.description: House Democrats greeted President Trump’s return to Congress on Tuesday with a spirited series of disruptive protests – and a number of painful looks as they suffered through an address in which the returning president repeatedly cajoled and mocked them.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump praises major government cutbacks, efficiency measures
item.description: The president singled out specific spending programs, including an $8 million program promoting LGBTQI+ initiatives in Lesotho.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful US storms kill 2 and bring threats from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms killed two people in Mississippi, tore the roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in a small town in Oklahoma and threatened more communities across the nation Tuesday with wide-ranging weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Joann store closures: See the list of nearly 800 locations headed for sale
item.description: After more than 80 years, national fabric and craft retailer Joann is another step closer to being erased from shopping centers across the U.S. after the release of nearly 800 locations going up for sale in the coming months.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration releases list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it has identified to close or sell, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: IRS drafting plans to cut as much as half of its 90,000-person workforce, AP sources say
item.description: The IRS is drafting plans to cut its workforce by as much as half through a mix of layoffs, attrition and incentivized buyouts, according to two people familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Driver surrenders to police after pursuit full of PIT maneuvers, standoffs
item.description: A Los Angeles Police pursuit in the San Fernando Valley was full of starts and stops Tuesday afternoon, but the driver eventually surrendered to police.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Carolina man allegedly assaults family member over 2 meats cooked for dinner
item.description: A family member who witnessed the incident told police that the man was upset about two meats being cooked for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, NewsNation

item.title: Florida man swallows $769K in stolen jewelry from Tiffany & Co.: police
item.description: A man was accused of stealing hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of jewelry from a Tiffany & Co. store in Orlando and swallowing the evidence.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Demonstrators across 50 states look to unify a disparate opposition to Trump and his sweeping agenda
item.description: As Donald Trump prepares to address a joint session of Congress, protest groups are gathering at parks, statehouses and other public grounds to assail his presidency as dangerous and un-American.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Charlotte Kramon And Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why Trump's speech to Congress isn't a 'State of the Union' address
item.description: It looks like the State of the Union, and will be carried on live television, just like those annual addresses are. But it's called something else: a joint address to Congress. And it has its origins in the first term of President Ronald Reagan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Former Los Angeles fire chief fails to get job back after ouster following historic deadly wildfire
item.description: The former Los Angeles fire chief ousted by Mayor Karen Bass after the most destructive wildfire in city history has failed to reclaim her job.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Body of snowboarder missing from Vail Mountain found weeks later, coroner says
item.description: The Eagle County Coroner's Office on Monday confirmed in an email Gill's body was found Friday, two weeks after he was last seen.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: AirPods dying? Professor discovers microenvironment that may be responsible
item.description: UT-Austin associate professor Yijin Lu said a similar microenvironment exists in all sorts of electronics. This includes places like your phone and electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Eric Henrikson, NewsNation

item.title: Fargo has an election method that helps mainstream candidates. North Dakota lawmakers may ban it
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — When Fargo voters approved a unique local election system in 2018, their goal was to choose candidates with broader support to lead North Dakota’s largest city. It worked — maybe too well.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: IHOP offers free short stack for National Pancake Day
item.description: Calling all pancake lovers! If you are ready to satisfy your craving, IHOP is celebrating National Pancake Day on March 4 with a free treat.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Columbia professor slams university leadership as anti-Israel agitators wreak havoc at Barnard
item.description: Columbia associate professor Shai Davidai criticizes leadership at Columbia and Barnard as anti-Israel agitators once again wreak havoc on campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Despite weather concerns, New Orleans celebrates Mardi Gras Day and bids farewell to Carnival season
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The good times and parades still rolled in New Orleans for Mardi Gras Day celebrations early Tuesday, ahead of expected severe winds, thunderstorms and tornado warnings in the afternoon.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Census Bureau under Trump seeks permission to delete questions about gender identity
item.description: The U.S. Census Bureau under the Trump administration has sought permission to delete questions about gender identity from a monthly survey that gathers near real-time data about American life.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jon Stewart accepts Elon Musk's demands for 'Daily Show' interview, fires back at his criticism
item.description: Jon Stewart is rejecting Elon Musk's criticism that he's a "propagandist," and added that he's "game" for an interview with the tech billionaire if he agrees to appear as a guest on "The Daily Show.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Red Robin considering closing dozens of 'underperforming' restaurants
item.description: Red Robin, which has locations in all but five states, says it's reviewing dozens of "underperforming" stores for potential closure.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pentagon orders thousands more troops to southern border
item.description: The Pentagon is sending up to 5,050 additional troops to the U.S.-Mexico border even as unlawful border crossings have sharply fallen in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google makes it easier to remove your personal info
item.description: Google is making removing your personal information from search results much easier. Now, when you search for yourself and see a link that contains information like your email, address or phone number, you can request a takedown in seconds.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: FAA investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching DC airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said Monday it is investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York prison guards fired for ignoring deal to end strike, thousands set to lose health insurance
item.description: New York officials have started to fire state prison guards who failed to comply with a deal to end their illegal labor strike, which is now in its third week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trans vegan cult member linked to Vermont border agent killing was person of interest in parents' murders
item.description: A double murder of a Pennsylvania couple in 2022 has been linked to the recent fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal agencies to review Columbia University's contracts, grants after 'failure' to protect Jewish students
item.description: Columbia University's contracts and grants are under review after it was accused of failing to protect Jewish students on campus during anti-Israel protests.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump vows to press ahead on reshaping America in speech to Congress as Democrats register dissent
item.description: President Donald Trump vowed to keep up his campaign of “swift and unrelenting action” in reorienting the nation’s economy, immigration and foreign policy as he delivered an unyielding address Tuesday night to Congress and the nation.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How South Carolina's execution of a condemned killer by firing squad will unfold
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When a South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat steps into the death row chamber Friday night, it won’t be lethal injection or electrocution that ends his life.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US stocks tumble after Trump announces Canadian and Mexican tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said tariffs he announced on Canada and Mexico will take effect on Tuesday. The bond market also took a hit following the latest warning signal on the strength of the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
